title: rubus micans subsp. pulcher
intro: Rubus micans subsp. pulcher is a plant species, also known as the spotted bramble, a type of raspberry native to the mountainous regions of the western Himalayas and Tibet. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, and is valued for its potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. The plant is typically prepared into herbal remedies, including decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions. Additionally, Rubus micans subsp. pulcher is also used in traditional Tibetan medicine for its anti-diarrheal and anti-spasmodic properties.
